title: Explicit heat grid step is locally L-infinity stable under the parabolic CFL condition

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If 0 <= r and 2r <= 1, and the three grid values u(i), u(i+1), and u(i+2) are bounded in absolute value by M, then the shifted heat grid update at i is also bounded by M.
informal.latex: If \(0\le r\), \(2r\le1\), and \(|u_i|,|u_{i+1}|,|u_{i+2}|\le M\), then \(|{\mathcal H}_r[u]_i|\le M\).
assumptions: r and M are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
i is a natural-number grid index
informal.expected_conclusion: The local heat grid update remains bounded by M in absolute value.

declarations.name: heatStepGrid
declarations.statement: def heatStepGrid (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := heatStep r (u i) (u (i + 1)) (u (i + 2))

declarations.name: heatStepGrid_abs_le
declarations.statement: theorem heatStepGrid_abs_le (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) (M : Real) (hr0 : 0 <= r) (hrCfl : 2 * r <= 1) (hLeft : |u i| <= M) (h : |u (i + 1)| <= M) (hRight : |u (i + 2)| <= M) : |heatStepGrid r u i| <= M

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: If 0 <= r and 2r <= 1, and the three grid values u(i), u(i+1), and u(i+2) are bounded in absolute value by M, then the shifted heat grid update at i is also bounded by M.
tasks.target: theorem heatStepGrid_abs_le (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) (M : Real) (hr0 : 0 <= r) (hrCfl : 2 * r <= 1) (hLeft : |u i| <= M) (h : |u (i + 1)| <= M) (hRight : |u (i + 2)| <= M) : |heatStepGrid r u i| <= M

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding heatStepGrid and applying heatStep_abs_le to the three neighboring grid values.

tasks.input: Find the scalar theorem used to prove local L-infinity stability of the heat grid-window update.
